data.title: H.B. Fuller outlines $4.55–$4.90 EPS guidance and mid-single digit revenue growth amid supply chain volatility
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: H.B. Fuller Company (FUL) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Celeste Mastin reported "we delivered on our profit commitment and executed with discipline in a challenging operating environment." Mastin highlighted that organic revenue decreased 6.6% year-on-year, with positive pricing offset by lower volume, but EBITDA of $119 million increased 4% year-on-year and EBITDA margin expanded 90 basis points to 15.4%.
  • Mastin detailed that the Engineering Adhesives (EA) segment achieved 3% organic growth (excluding solar), while HHC declined 10%, and BAS sales decreased 5.1%. Segment EBITDA margins improved, particularly in EA and HHC due to restructuring savings and positive price actions.
  • Mastin described significant supply chain disruptions from the Middle East conflict, noting, "we have received over 40 force majeure letters from suppliers in recent weeks, clear evidence that this is a major disruption." In response, H.B. Fuller announced "a minimum 10% price increase across all product lines globally effective April 1 with significantly higher price adjustments for certain technologies and regions where cost escalation is more acute."
  • Mastin emphasized that "this disruption creates a unique opportunity to support existing customers and gain market share, positioning us for improved volume growth in the future."
  • CFO John Corkrean stated, "Adjusted gross profit margin was 31.3%, up 170 basis points versus last year as positive pricing and raw material actions as well as restructuring savings more than offset volume declines." Corkrean also reported "adjusted earnings per share of $0.57 was up 6% versus the same quarter in 2025 driven by higher operating income and lower shares outstanding."
  • Corkrean shared, "Net debt to adjusted EBITDA was 3.1x, consistent with fiscal year-end 2025 and down from 3.5x at the end of the first quarter of last year."

Outlook

  • Corkrean announced, "Net revenue is now expected to be up mid-single digits and organic revenue is now expected to be up low single digits versus fiscal 2025, reflecting updated pricing actions and anticipated market share gains."
  • Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2026 is now expected to be in the range of $645 million to $675 million. Adjusted EPS is forecasted in the range of $4.55 to $4.90. For the second quarter, net revenue is expected to be up low single digits and adjusted EBITDA is expected in the range of $175 million to $185 million.
  • Corkrean indicated a temporary pause on closing acquisitions, reallocating capital to share repurchases while maintaining a target of 2.5x to 3x net debt to EBITDA.

Financial Results

  • Organic revenue decreased 6.6% year-on-year; pricing was up 0.6%, volume down 7.2%.
  • Currency had a positive impact of 3.6% and acquisitions added 0.7% to revenue.
  • Adjusted SG&A expense was up 4% year-over-year, but excluding acquisitions and foreign exchange, SG&A was down slightly year-on-year.
  • Cash flow from operations improved $49 million year-on-year.

Q&A

  • Patrick Cunningham, Citigroup: Asked about the 10% price hike and guidance raise amid raw material constraints. Mastin explained price increases are a response to "the market's really squeezed" and that "we believe this market reset is a very sustainable place for us to be," noting long-term supply chain changes.
  • Michael Harrison, Seaport: Asked about Lunar New Year’s impact and activity in China. Mastin said, "we experienced about a $15 million to $20 million revenue impact from Chinese New Year in Q1" and noted post-holiday, "our HHC business did very well in Asia Pacific."
  • Lucas Beaumont, UBS: Inquired about further pricing actions. Mastin confirmed, "10% was a minimum that we needed to do across all materials," and additional price supplements are being negotiated.
  • Kevin McCarthy, Vertical: Sought detail on share gain opportunities. Mastin emphasized "quite a number of opportunities to gain share in HHC, certainly, particularly because a lot of the raw material base for HHC comes out of China," and Corkrean added, "we are asking them to sign longer-term agreements."
  • Jeffrey Zekauskas, JPMorgan: Asked about volume outlook changes. Mastin cited "positive impact of gaining share" offset by "customers potentially not being able to get other materials" and "erosion in global demand because of this inflationary environment."
  • Emily Fusco, Deutsche Bank: Questioned order trends in March. Mastin reported "improved -- higher revenue" and "improved margins" in March, with no evidence of customer hoarding.
  • Rosemarie Morbelli, Gabelli: Asked about medical-grade adhesives. Mastin said, "our medical business in Europe was up almost 20%, again, organically," but noted the segment remains small. On solar, Mastin stated, "we're already at the trough revenue we expect there," with recovery expected by the third quarter.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a proactive and occasionally skeptical tone, pressing on sustainability of price hikes, volume declines, and supply chain risks.
  • Management’s tone was confident during prepared remarks, repeatedly referencing discipline, agility, and market opportunity, while in Q&A, responses showed candor about uncertainties, but maintained a constructive outlook. Mastin used phrases such as "we believe this market reset is a very sustainable place for us to be" and "we're being very selective about where to gain share."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s sentiment shifted to a more urgent and opportunity-seeking tone, driven by supply chain disruptions and aggressive pricing.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance raised from flat to up 2% net revenue (previous quarter) to mid-single digit growth; organic revenue guidance moved from flat to low single digits.
  • Adjusted EPS guidance increased from a range of $4.35 to $4.70 to $4.55 to $4.90, and EBITDA guidance lifted from $630 million–$660 million to $645 million–$675 million.
  • Previous quarter focused on portfolio repositioning and cost structure, while the current quarter underscores rapid response to supply chain disruptions and market share capture.
  • Analysts in the current quarter are more focused on the sustainability of price and volume assumptions amid external shocks.
  • Management’s tone has shifted from steady progress to assertive action, highlighting supply chain resilience and market share as key priorities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Mastin warned of "significant broad-based inflationary pressure and raw material shortages" resulting from the Middle East conflict.
  • Over 40 force majeure notices have been received from suppliers, signaling major disruption in raw material supply.
  • Corkrean indicated managing inventory will be "a little trickier in this environment," and that the "biggest question will probably be around inventory management."
  • Mastin anticipated potential customer challenges in sourcing non-adhesive inputs, which could negatively impact demand, though no such impact was seen yet.

Final Takeaway

H.B. Fuller management delivered an assertive message that, despite lower volumes and supply chain disruptions, decisive early pricing actions and a robust global sourcing strategy are expected to preserve profitability and support mid-single digit revenue growth for 2026. The company is leveraging current market volatility to pursue share gains, maintain margin expansion, and strengthen long-term competitiveness, while proactively managing risks associated with raw material shortages and inflation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Macy's sees strong results from tests of its Gemini-powered chatbot
data.description:
Macy's (M) saw positive results from early tests of its Google Gemini-powered chatbot.

Notably, Macy's (M) customers who use the Ask Macy's chatbot have spent around 4.75 times more than those who don’t, according to company data over the last few weeks. The department store operator is testing the shopping assistant with about half of the visitors to its website.

The AI shopping assistant powered by Google’s Gemini platform lets shoppers ask product questions in natural language and get responses without leaving the Macy’s site. Gemini interprets the intent, and Macy’s (M) returns relevant product guidance or answers. 

The retail sector in general has seen strong results from the implementation of AI chatbots. Conversion rates on shopping sites have improved, and some companies have been able to reduce headcount dedicated to customer service.

Shares of Macy's (M) dipped 0.5% in Thursday trading and are down more than 16% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Ukraine takes drone defense pitch to Gulf in bid for military support
data.description:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy made an unannounced trip to the Persian Gulf this week, aiming to trade battlefield expertise in countering drones for additional defense assistance, according to press reports.

Arriving in Saudi Arabia, Zelenskiy said he planned to hold key discussions with regional leaders, emphasizing cooperation on security issues. He offered few specifics about the broader itinerary, while the visit will include multiple stops across the region.

Ukraine is expected to finalize an air defense agreement with Saudi Arabia, according to a report by Agence France-Presse citing a senior official.

Kyiv has increasingly positioned its experience combating drone warfare as a strategic asset. Since the escalation of conflict involving Iran, Ukrainian teams have been deployed to Gulf countries to advise on defending critical infrastructure from aerial attacks that have disrupted energy supplies.

In return, Ukraine is seeking greater access to advanced U.S.-made missile systems, which remain among the few tools capable of intercepting high-speed ballistic threats from Russia.

Zelenskiy was joined on the trip by Defense Minister Rustem Umerov, who previously led a delegation to the region earlier this month. Ukraine has already sent more than 200 military personnel to assist several Middle Eastern nations in strengthening their air defenses against Iranian drone strikes.

The diplomatic push comes at a challenging moment for Kyiv. Efforts to secure new weapons commitments from NATO allies have slowed, while a proposed €90 billion European Union loan package remains stalled due to opposition from Viktor Orbán’s government in Hungary.

data.title: Banxico lowers policy rate despite war-linked inflation risks
data.description:
Mexico's central bank on Thursday pulled down its benchmark rate by 25 basis points to 6.75%, choosing to keep easing even as inflation has picked up and the Iran war threatens another round of energy-driven price pressures. 

Banxico signaled concern over a weakening economy even as consumer inflation continues to accelerate. In their statement, policymakers said they are considering one additional rate cut at an unspecified time, noting they will monitor external conditions and the effects of all factors influencing inflation.

The split-vote decision came just days after Mexico’s annual inflation jumped more than expected, reaching 4.63% in the first half of March. Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and fuel prices, slowed only slightly to 4.46%. The central bank targets inflation of 3%, plus or minus one percentage point. 

Last month, policymakers had paused a nearly two-year easing cycle by holding rates steady at 7%.

Board member Jonathan Heath dissented, arguing that signaling possible rate cuts would be premature given current inflation projections, according to the latest Banxico minutes.

Banxico's cut stands in stark contrast to the broader mood among global central banks, with many policymakers elsewhere turning more cautious as the war pushes up oil prices and forces the market to scale back rate-cut expectations. 

In the U.S. (SPY) (VOO), traders have sharply pared Fed easing bets for 2026, with traders now increasingly pricing in rate hikes, while officials at the European Central Bank (VGK) (IEV) and Bank of England (EWU) (FLGB) have stressed vigilance and patience as they assess the inflation fallout.

Mexico ETFs: (EWW), (FLMX), and (MEXX).

data.title: Enerpac Tool sinks to 52-wk low as margin pressure, weak service business cloud outlook
data.description:
Enerpac Tool (EPAC) shares dropped more than 8% Thursday to a 52-week low, even after the industrial tools maker reported results that topped Wall Street expectations on revenue and met profit estimates.

Enerpac Tool (EPAC) posted net sales of about $155 million, exceeding analyst forecasts of $147 million, while adjusted earnings of $0.39 a share were in line with consensus for its fiscal second quarter.

Despite the headline beat, investors appeared focused on deteriorating profitability and signs of underlying weakness in key parts of the business.

Profit declines, margin compression

Net income fell to $16.3 million, or $0.31 a share, from $20.9 million, or $0.38 a share, a year earlier.

Gross profit margins dropped sharply, declining more than four percentage points year over year to 46.4%, reflecting continued pressure in the company’s service segment. Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization totaled $33.0 million, down from $33.8 million a year earlier, with margins also slipping.

The company’s service business, particularly in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, was a notable weak spot. Service revenue declined 17% organically, as softer demand weighed on results.

Higher costs also pressured profitability, with selling, general and administrative expenses rising due in part to restructuring charges tied to efforts to address that weakness.

Guidance signals limited growth

Enerpac (EPAC) reaffirmed full-year expectations but offered a relatively muted outlook. The company forecast net sales of $635 million to $650 million, roughly in line with consensus estimates of $637.1 million, and organic growth of just 1% to 3%.

It projected full-year adjusted ebitda of $158 million to $163 million and adjusted earnings of $1.85 to $1.92 a share, with the high end only matching analyst expectations. Free cash flow guidance remained unchanged at $100 million to $110 million.

data.title: Worst performing Mortgage REITs in the past one month
data.description:
Freddie Mac's latest Primary Mortgage Market Survey shows 30-year fixed‑rate mortgages climbing to 6.38% as of March 26, up 16 basis points from last week's 6.22% but still well below the 6.65% recorded a year earlier.

Despite the uptick, the housing market continues to improve gradually, with year‑over‑year gains in purchase and refinance applications signaling a more affordable spring buying season ahead.

In light of this, below is a list of the 10 worst performing Mortgage REIT stocks in the past month. The list is ranked based on lowest one month price performance percentage.

The list is topped by AGNC Investment Corp. (AGNC), with a one month price performance of -10.19%. Rithm Capital Corp. (RITM) and Dynex Capital, Inc. (DX) are next, with ARMOUR Residential REIT, Inc. (ARR) and NexPoint Real Estate Finance, Inc. (NREF) rounding out the rest of the top five.

The remaining five stocks on the list include Redwood Trust, Inc. (RWT), Adamas Trust, Inc. (ADAM), and Annaly Capital Management, Inc. (NLY). KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF) and Claros Mortgage Trust, Inc. (CMTG) complete the bottom of the list, with declines ranging from approximately -10.19% to -6.47%.

Here is the list:

  1. AGNC Investment Corp. (AGNC), 1 month performance percentage: -10.19%

  2. Rithm Capital Corp. (RITM), 1 month performance percentage: -9.59%

  3. Dynex Capital, Inc. (DX), 1 month performance percentage: -9.10%

  4. ARMOUR Residential REIT, Inc. (ARR), 1 month performance percentage: -8.94%

  5. NexPoint Real Estate Finance, Inc. (NREF), 1 month performance percentage: -8.55%

  6. Redwood Trust, Inc. (RWT), 1 month performance percentage: -7.93%

  7. Adamas Trust, Inc. (ADAM), 1 month performance percentage: -7.61%

  8. Annaly Capital Management, Inc. (NLY), 1 month performance percentage: -6.95%

  9. KKR Real Estate Finance Trust Inc. (KREF), 1 month performance percentage: -6.55%

  10. Claros Mortgage Trust, Inc. (CMTG), 1 month performance percentage: -6.47%

Real Estate ETFs: (VNQ), (XLRE), (IYR), (USRT), and (HOMZ)

data.title: Occidental Petroleum CEO Hollub set to step down later this year - Reuters
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum (OXY) President and CEO Vicki Hollub is preparing to retire and plans to make a formal ‌announcement later this year, likely to be succeeded by Richard Jackson, who was elevated to COO in October, Reuters reported Thursday.

A transition period is expected, which could include Hollub remaining in an advisory capacity to the new CEO, or announcing she will step down at a future date, with ​Jackson named CEO-designate, according to the report, which also said Hollub likely would remain ​on the company's board.

Hollub's exit would mark an end to a career at Oxy (OXY) that has spanned more than 40 years, including the last 10 as CEO, which made her the first woman to head a major oil company.

Her tenure has been highlighted by the $55B debt-fueled 2019 acquisition of rival Anadarko Petroleum, completed in part with $10B in financing ​from Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway; since the deal's closure in August 2019, Oxy (OXY) shares have gained ~35% vs. a 122% rise in the S&P 500 index.

data.title: Precious metals are failing to be safe havens during market turmoil – Inside Edge Capital
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has lost its status as a safe haven and is now behaving more like a risk asset, according to Todd Gordon, founder of Inside Edge Capital.

The precious metal, which saw a record run earlier this year, has been falling alongside stocks rather than providing investors with a hedge against market volatility and geopolitical uncertainty surrounding the Iran war.

In an interview with CNBC, Gordon explained that rising interest rates are the primary culprit behind gold’s struggles.

With elevated expectations for inflation driven by embedded energy costs, real rates have climbed and fundamentally changed the investment case for precious metals, he said.

The timeline for Federal Reserve rate cuts has also shifted dramatically, further undermining gold’s (XAUUSD:CUR) appeal. Gordon noted that the market isn’t “expecting a rate cut until two Halloweens from now,” a stark change from earlier expectations.

This removal of near-term monetary easing has prompted Gordon to reduce his exposure to the metals trade.

Gold mining stocks (GDX), which are heavily leveraged to gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR), have become particularly vulnerable in Gordon’s view. He pointed out that the GDX, the popular gold miners ETF, has surged 180% over the past year and is now “really overbought.”

Gordon has been cutting specific holdings, including AngloGold (AU), Agnico Eagle (AEM), and Kinross (KGC), from his portfolio.

Instead of precious metals, Gordon is pivoting toward the energy sector (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), where he sees stronger fundamentals. He argued that structural demand for fossil fuels will likely persist longer than many investors previously anticipated.

Beyond gold, Gordon is also trimming his exposure to silver (XAGUSD:CUR), though he maintains a more nuanced view on copper miners (COPX).

His overall message is clear: in the current interest rate environment, precious metals are failing to deliver the safe haven benefits that investors have historically relied upon during periods of market stress and geopolitical turmoil.

Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST).

Silver and Silver Mining ETFs: (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), (PSLV), (SIL), (SILJ), and (SLVP).

Copper and Copper Mining ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), and (JJCTF).

data.title: 7-Eleven has a lofty goal to reach 100K stores globally
data.description:
Seven & i Holdings (SVNDY) plans to expand its 7-Eleven store network in Asia, Europe, and other markets as it attempts to strike the right balance between utilizing its Japanese franchise model and U.S. operations with the needs of local markets.

7-Eleven still has the most stores in Japan by a wide margin. After Japan, the biggest store counts are in South Korea, the United States, and Thailand. Looking ahead, Seven & i (SVNDY) has a target to increase its stores worldwide to 100K by 2030, an increase of 14,000 from the current number. Since the Japanese and U.S. markets are maturing, global expansion is considered key to reaching the lofty goal.

"Looking at a world map, there are many countries where we haven't yet entered," stated 7-Eleven International Chairman Shinji Abe. "There's still room for growth," he added.

Seven & i Holdings (SVNDY) has been planning a U.S. IPO for its North American convenience-store business, with multiple reports saying the listing is targeted for the second half of 2026. That plan is part of a broader restructuring to unlock shareholder value and fund faster growth, including more U.S. store openings and possible acquisitions.

data.title: Israel reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Israel press release (IRLCF): Q4 net loss of $37M
  • As of December 31, 2025, total financial liabilities were $679 million, and investments in liquid assets amounted to $721 million.
  • Net cash[1] as of December 31, 2025, totaled $73 million. The net cash includes the fair value of derivative transactions, which decreases the economic value of the financial liabilities by $31 million. As of September 30, 2025, the net cash was $45 million.
